# services.keter

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.keter`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.keter.bundle.appName` | `string` | The name keter assigns to this bundle |
| `services.keter.bundle.domain` | `string` | The domain keter will bind to |
| `services.keter.bundle.executable` | `absolute path` | The executable to be run |
| `services.keter.bundle.publicScript` | `string` | Allows loading of public environment variables, these are emitted to the log so it shouldn't contain secrets. |
| `services.keter.bundle.secretScript` | `string` | Allows loading of private environment variables |
| `services.keter.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable keter, a web app deployment manager. Note that this module only support loading of webapps: Keep an old app running and swap the ports when the new one is booted . |
| `services.keter.globalKeterConfig` | `open submodule of (YAML 1.1 value)` | Global config for keter, see <https://github.com/snoyberg/keter/blob/master/etc/keter-config.yaml> for reference |
| `services.keter.globalKeterConfig.ip-from-header` | `boolean` | You want that ip-from-header in the nginx setup case. It allows nginx setting the original ip address rather then it being localhost (due to reverse proxying) |
| `services.keter.globalKeterConfig.listeners` | `list of (submodule)` | You want that ip-from-header in the nginx setup case. It allows nginx setting the original ip address rather then it being localhost (due to reverse proxying). However if you configure keter to accept connections directly you may want to set this to false. |
| `services.keter.globalKeterConfig.listeners.*.host` | `string` | host |
| `services.keter.globalKeterConfig.listeners.*.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | port |
| `services.keter.globalKeterConfig.rotate-logs` | `boolean` | emits keter logs and it's applications to stderr. which allows journald to capture them. Set to true to let keter put the logs in files (useful on non systemd systems, this is the old approach where keter handled log management) |
| `services.keter.package` | `package` | The keter package to use. |
| `services.keter.root` | `string` | Mutable state folder for keter |
